title: More US visa applicants in PH must set social media to public - Inquirer.net
description: More US visa applicants in PH must set social media to public  Inquirer.netState Department Expands ‘Online Presence Review’ for Additional Nonimmigrant Visas Effective March 30  natlawreview.comPriest, spouses, and trafficking victims are now subjected to social media vetting for US visas  ThePrintH1B Visa: US extends social media screening rules covering H-1B, student, and other nonimmigrant visas  timesofindia.indiatimes.comUS to expand social media checks to more visa categories from March 30  The Economic Times
